title: OpsGenie Alert
description: Represents an alert in the OpsGenie incident management platform. Alerts are the primary mechanism for notifying teams about issues that require attention.

id.description: Unique identifier of the alert assigned by OpsGenie.

tinyId.description: Short, human-readable identifier for the alert.

alias.description: Client-defined identifier used for alert deduplication.

message.description: The alert message, limited to 130 characters.

description.description: Detailed description providing context about the alert.

acknowledged.description: Whether the alert has been acknowledged by a responder.

isSeen.description: Whether the alert has been viewed.

snoozed.description: Whether the alert is currently snoozed.

snoozedUntil.description: Date and time when the snooze period ends.

tags.description: Tags attached to the alert for categorization.

count.description: Number of times the alert has occurred (deduplication count).

lastOccurredAt.description: Date and time of the most recent occurrence.

createdAt.description: Date and time when the alert was first created.

updatedAt.description: Date and time of the last modification.

source.description: Source of the alert, typically the integration or tool that created it.

owner.description: Username of the alert owner (assigned user).

priority.description: Priority level from P1 (critical) to P5 (informational).

entity.description: Entity field used to specify the domain of the alert.

responders.description: Teams, users, escalations, and schedules responsible for the alert.

visibleTo.description: Teams and users who can see the alert without receiving notifications.

actions.description: Custom actions available for the alert.

details.description: Custom key-value pairs providing additional context.

Responder.description: A team, user, escalation, or schedule that responds to alerts.

name.description: Name of the responder (teams and schedules).

Integration.description: The integration that created the alert.

AlertReport.description: Report containing timing and actor information for the alert lifecycle.

ackTime.description: Time in milliseconds from creation to acknowledgment.

closeTime.description: Time in milliseconds from creation to closure.

acknowledgedBy.description: Username of the person who acknowledged the alert.

closedBy.description: Username of the person who closed the alert.
